@@ -10,12 +10,19 @@
   "created_at": "2021-12-08T09:34:09Z",
   "github_updated_at": "2022-01-21T09:52:47Z",
   "body_snippet": "


## Current Behavior
When sharing a config file between two computers it needs to be exported from computer A to Computer B.
You then have to import the config file -> delete the old version -> fix the folder structure (or else you end up with a lot of nested folders).


## Solution: 
Add an option to enable auto export to file whenever a setting have been edited. 
Config info could be saved inside the config file.

Add an option to merge all changes from config file. Either automa...",

@@ -86,5 +93,5 @@

-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): This issue proposes new functionalities (merging and auto-exporting configuration files) that would enhance user experience and data management by providing more flexible ways to manage connection data.
Approach: Implement a 'MergeConnectionsFromFile' function to deserialize connections from a specified file and integrate them into the active connection tree, including conflict resolution options. For auto-export, create an 'AutoConnectionsExporter' service or timed event within 'FrmMain' to periodically save the connection tree using 'XmlConnectionsSaver', configurable via new settings."
 }

   "github_updated_at": "2026-02-21T10:23:20Z",
   "body_snippet": "## Expected Behavior\r\nEither create a new setting in the config for a folder or if a period is used for domain, don't use the current hostname that mRemoteNG is running on as the value of the domain setting.\r\n\r\n## Current Behavior\r\nAFAIK, current configuration doesn't allow for setting the domain of the connection to be the same as the name of the connection\r\nSetting a period as the value of the domain setting will use the name of the current host that mRemoteNG is running on as the value and no...",

+      "seq": 1,
+      "date": "2026-02-21",
+      "type": "triaged",
+      "description": "Allows users to dynamically set the domain value based on the connection name, enhancing flexibility."
+    }
+  ],
   "comments": [
     {
       "id": "IC_kwDOAAcIMM7mXEEX",
@@ -51,5 +58,5 @@
   "needs_action": false,
   "waiting_for_us": false,
   "last_synced": "2026-02-21T12:06:48Z",
-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): Allows users to dynamically set the domain value based on the connection name, enhancing flexibility.\nApproach: Introduce a new method `ExpandDomainVariables` in `ConnectionInfo.cs` to replace a placeholder (e.g., `%Name%` or `%ConnectionName%`) in the 'Domain' property with the connection's actual name. Integrate this method into the getter of the 'Domain' property in `ConnectionInfo.cs`. Add a new unit test in `ConnectionInfoTests.cs` to verify this functionality."
 }

diff --git a/mRemoteNG/Connection/AbstractConnectionRecord.cs b/mRemoteNG/Connection/AbstractConnectionRecord.cs
index 9591c1938..77f6e3bd1 100644
--- a/mRemoteNG/Connection/AbstractConnectionRecord.cs
+++ b/mRemoteNG/Connection/AbstractConnectionRecord.cs
@@ -282,6 +282,20 @@ namespace mRemoteNG.Connection
             return raw.Replace("%name%", _name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
         }
 
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Expands domain tokens to the connection's <see cref="Name"/> value.
+        /// Supported tokens: <c>%name%</c>, <c>%connectionname%</c>.
+        /// </summary>
+        private string ExpandDomainVariables(string raw)
+        {
+            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(raw) || !raw.Contains('%'))
+                return raw;
+
+            return raw
+                .Replace("%name%", _name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
+                .Replace("%connectionname%", _name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
+        }
+
         [LocalizedAttributes.LocalizedCategory(nameof(Language.Connection), 2),
          DisplayName("IP Address"),
          Description("IP address for this connection. When 'Primary Address' is set to 'IP Address', this is used for connecting instead of the Hostname field."),
@@ -412,7 +426,7 @@ namespace mRemoteNG.Connection
          AttributeUsedInProtocol(ProtocolType.RDP, ProtocolType.IntApp, ProtocolType.PowerShell, ProtocolType.WSL)]
         public string Domain
         {
-            get => GetPropertyValue("Domain", _domain)?.Trim() ?? string.Empty;
+            get => ExpandDomainVariables(GetPropertyValue("Domain", _domain)?.Trim() ?? string.Empty);
             set => SetField(ref _domain, value?.Trim() ?? string.Empty, "Domain");
         }
